member.name: Sir Ashley Fox
member.synopsis: Sir Ashley Fox is the Conservative MP for Bridgwater , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Opposition Assistant Whip (Commons).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: CCTV
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 26 June 2026 to Question 10713 on Railways: CCTV, whether the Network Rail programme will connect all Network Rail-managed station CCTV into the single cloud-based system; and what cat
writtenQuestions.answerText: Further to the Answer of 26 June 2026 to Question 10713, the rail CCTV connectivity project is in its start-up phase. Network Rail is delivering the project on behalf of the rail industry and will use an agreed model, currently being developed, to priorit...

writtenQuestions.heading: International Broadcasting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, pursuant to the Answer of 25 June 2026 to Question 11747 on International Broadcasting, whether any Minister in her Department has had discussions with stakeholders on Wester
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) is not aware of any such discussions, but details of all ministerial meetings with external organisations are routinely published by the FCDO in its quarterly transparency releases.

writtenQuestions.heading: Maritime and Coastguard Agency: British Overseas Territories
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 11742 on British Overseas Territories, what estimate she has made of the cost to the public purse of each monitoring visit undertaken by officials from the Mar
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.heading: Syria: Humanitarian Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, how much of the £250 million in humanitarian assistance being given to Syria will directly or indirectly go towards supporting the inclusion of Syrian minority communities, a
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 22 June in response to Question 10866 and to my written statement to the House on 29 April.

writtenQuestions.heading: Syria: Christianity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether her Department has made an assessment of the level of violence faced by converted Christians in Syria.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 22 June in response to Question 10866 and to my written statement to the House on 29 April.

writtenQuestions.heading: Syria: Christianity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether her Department has made an assessment of how the UK can support the prevention of further violence against Christians in Syria.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 22 June in response to Question 10866 and to my written statement to the House on 29 April.

writtenQuestions.heading: Human Rights and Religious Freedom
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, if she will include Freedom of Religion or Belief in any future Human Rights and Democracy Reports.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 19 June in response to Question 9575.

writtenQuestions.heading: Arms Length Bodies: Lobbying
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether Cabinet Office guidance permits arm's length bodies to use public funds to appoint external public affairs or lobbying consultancies to engage with ministers, government departments or Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office does not issue specific guidance on this matter. However, the Code of Conduct for Board Members of Public Bodies states that ‘it will always be an improper use of public funds for public bodies to employ consultants or other companies t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact on UK vehicle manufacturing of the Rules of Origin requirements for electric vehicles and batteries due to apply from 1 January 2027 unde
writtenQuestions.answerText: New rules of origin requirements for electric vehicles under the TCA are scheduled to come into force on 1st January 2027. The UK Government recognises and shares the growing concern from industry about the potential impact of the upcoming rule changes on...

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving: Qualifications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the potential merits of simplifying or consolidating the statutory qualification and certification requirements for professional bus, coach and heavy goods vehicle dri
writtenQuestions.answerText: A review in 2021/22 concluded that the Driver Certificate of Professional Competence (CPC) should be retained but reformed. A package of reforms was introduced in December 2024 to provide greater flexibility for drivers and operators. The Driver and Vehic...

writtenQuestions.heading: Shipping: Crew
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what discussions Ministers and officials have had since 4 July 2024 with representatives of the maritime industry, maritime training providers and trade unions on barriers to employing 16 and 17-year-old seafar
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers and officials regularly engage with the maritime industry, maritime training providers and trade unions. 16 and 17-year-olds wishing to become seafarers can undertake training to do so. The International Convention on Standards of Training, Cert...

writtenQuestions.heading: Vans: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what estimate her Department has made of the proportion of light commercial vans that will require at least one enroute charging stop during a typical working day following the transition to zero emission vans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made an estimate of the proportion of light commercial vans (LCV) that would require a charging stop during a typical working day. The charging requirements of an electric LCV depend on a wide range of factors, including vehicle mod...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Crew
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many people (a) enrolled on and (b) failed to complete commercial pilot training courses in the UK in each of the last five years; and what the corresponding completion rate was in each year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Nationalisation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 22 June 2026 to Question 10032, what proportion of the expected savings from public ownership are attributable to management fees.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Moving train operating companies back into public ownership will end decades of fragmentation and waste. Train operators in public ownership are beginning to work closer together with Network Rail, forming integrated railways and shifting from adversarial...

writtenQuestions.heading: Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 22 June 2026 to Question 8651 on Large Goods Vehicles: Electric Vehicles, whether her Department has undertaken modelling of the impact of charging times on the operational efficiency
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department will continue to engage with the sector on issues relating to efficiency and productivity, including the implications of charging. We will draw on the detailed data that will be generated and shared as part of the Zero Emission HGV and Infr...

writtenQuestions.heading: National Highways: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 12 June 2026 to Question 5451 on National Highways: Expenditure, how many full-time equivalent staff were employed within National Highways' external affairs function on (a) 4 July 202
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 4 July 2024 the National Highways external affairs team had 10.9 full-time equivalent (FTE) staff in post. The team was at that time carrying a number of vacancies out of its total headcount of 19 FTE. The National Highways external affairs team curren...

writtenQuestions.heading: South West Main Line: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what the cancellation rate was for South Western Railway services in (a) the 12 months before 25 May 2025 and (b) the most recent 12-month period for which data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: SWR's All Stations Cancellations at the end of May 2025 was 2.85%. The same metric at the end of May 2026 was 3.03%. SWR inherited delays to the introduction of the new Arterio fleet and a shortage of trained drivers, which has affected service resilience...

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Routes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the impact of specialist workforce shortages on delivery of the Airspace Modernisation Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department recognises the importance of specialist skills in delivering modernised airspace and is working closely with the Civil Aviation Authority to monitor and address any potential shortages. The new UK Airspace Design Service will help focus res...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Scooters: Road Traffic Offences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many people were convicted of offences relating to the use of privately-owned e-scooters on public roads in each police force area in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport does not hold this data.

writtenQuestions.heading: Liverpool Street Station: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what estimate has been made of the potential impact of the Liverpool Street Station redevelopment scheme on passenger capacity during construction, including the proportion of concourse space and platforms expe
writtenQuestions.answerText: Maintaining services during construction is a priority. The proposed phased approach keeps the station operational throughout. While concourse capacity will be reduced in phases during construction, passenger footfall has been modelled against this to ens...
